messages.content:
Translate to Portuguese (Brazilian)

"Our child will be beautiful, Isaac."
"Nosso filho será lindo, Isaac."

Translate to Portuguese (Brazilian)

"It amuses me."
"Isso me diverte."

Translate to Portuguese (Brazilian)

"Oh, hello Doug I didn't see you."
"Ah, olá Doug, eu não te vi."

Translate to Portuguese (Brazilian)

"Fifty-Five Night's at Cixi's"
"Cinquenta e Cinco Noites na Cixi's"

Translate to Portuguese (Brazilian)

"Fifty-Five Night's at Cixi's place"
"Cinquenta e Cinco Noites na casa de Cixi"

Translate to English
Cinquenta e Cinco Noites na de Cixi"
"Fifty-Five Nights at Cixi's"

Translate to Spanish

"Jesus in Vietnam"
"Jesús en Vietnam"
"Joke Talk"
"Charla de Bromas"

Translate to English

"Elpidio Valdés: La Película"
"Elpidio Valdés: The Movie"

Translate to English

"A los pioneros, que de haber vivido en eltiempo de los mamablses, habrian cargado al machete como el mas pinto de la paloma."

"To the pioneers, who if they had lived in the time of the mamablses, would have carried the machete like the most painted of the dove." 

(Note: "mamablses" seems to be a typographical error or a word not recognized in Spanish. The phrase "el más pinto de la paloma" is also not a standard Spanish idiom, so the translation for that part may be approximate. Context could change the meaning of the phrase.)
